paper_metadata.title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners
paper_metadata.abstract:
This paper explores a simple method for improving the zero-shot learning abilities of language models. We show that instruction tuning -- finetuning language models on a collection of tasks described via instructions -- substantially improves zero-shot performance on unseen tasks.
  We take a 137B parameter pretrained language model and instruction-tune it on over 60 NLP tasks verbalized via natural language instruction templates. We evaluate this instruction-tuned model, which we call FLAN, on unseen task types. FLAN substantially improves the performance of its unmodified counterpart and surpasses zero-shot 175B GPT-3 on 20 of 25 tasks that we evaluate. FLAN even outperforms few-shot GPT-3 by a large margin on ANLI, RTE, BoolQ, AI2-ARC, OpenbookQA, and StoryCloze. Ablation studies reveal that number of finetuning datasets, model scale, and natural language instructions are key to the success of instruction tuning.

extraction_data.innovation: Instruction tuning, which finetunes a pretrained language model on a collection of datasets described via instructions, substantially improves zero-shot performance on unseen tasks. This method enables the model to generalize to task types not seen during instruction tuning.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Instruction tuning on over 60 NLP datasets verbalized via natural language instructions
extraction_data.extension: Natural language instruction templates for verbalizing NLP tasks, enabling zero-shot generalization to unseen task types.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving zero-shot learning abilities of language models
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot task transfer, natural language inference, reading comprehension, closed-book question answering, commonsense reasoning, translation, sentiment analysis, coreference resolution
extraction_data.paper_title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners

extraction_data.innovation: Instruction fine-tuning on a diverse set of tasks enables zero-shot generalization to unseen tasks, improving performance on a wide range of benchmarks without task-specific training.
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot task transfer, question answering, text classification, natural language inference, summarization
extraction_data.research_problem: Zero-shot learning and generalization in language models
extraction_data.extension: Instruction tuning across a diverse set of tasks to enable zero-shot generalization.
extraction_data.paper_title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners

extraction_data.innovation: Extends instruction fine-tuning to encoder-decoder architectures, leveraging T5's pretraining objective for improved zero-shot performance on generative tasks.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Mixture of tasks including CoQA, SQuAD, and others
extraction_data.extension: Multi-task instruction fine-tuning across 12 task clusters and over 60 datasets to improve zero-shot performance on unseen tasks.
extraction_data.research_problem: Zero-shot learning and task generalization in language models
extraction_data.application: Zero-shot task generalization, instruction following, question answering, text classification
extraction_data.paper_title: Finetuned Language Models Are Zero-Shot Learners
